claim_text: Age-related changes in bulk gene expression across tissues are attributable to both changes in the numbers of cells of each type in a population and changes in per-cell gene expression levels.
evidence_location: Main text page 590; Extended Data Fig. 5a-b
supporting_quote: we observed that-in many cases-changes in gene expression are due to both changes in the numbers of cells in a population and changes in the gene expression levels in each cell

claim_text: The proportion of cells expressing the senescence marker Cdkn2a more than doubled in older mice compared with younger mice, confirmed by both FACS and droplet single-cell sequencing platforms.
evidence_location: Main text page 590-591; Fig. 2a-b
supporting_quote: The proportion of cells expressing Cdkn2a more than doubled in older mice compared with younger mice according to analysis by both FACS (Fig. 2a) and droplet (Fig. 2b) methods
key_terms: Cdkn2a: cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor 2A gene encoding the senescence marker protein p16

claim_text: Among previously characterized senescence markers, Cdkn2a shows the highest correlation between ageing and the fraction of cells in which it is expressed.
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Supplementary Table 4
supporting_quote: Cdkn2a has the highest correlation between ageing and the fraction of cells in which it is expressed
key_terms: Cdkn2a: cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor 2A gene encoding the senescence marker protein p16

claim_text: The bladder undergoes pronounced age-related compositional changes: the mesenchymal compartment decreases by approximately a factor of three over the mouse lifespan while the urothelial compartment increases by a similar amount.
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Fig. 2e
supporting_quote: The bladder shows pronounced changes in cell-type composition with age (Fig. 2e): whereas the mesenchymal compartment of this tissue decreases by a factor of three over the lifetime of the mouse (Fig. 2e, left), the urothelial compartment increases by a similar amount (Fig. 2e, right).

claim_text: The relative abundance of mesangial cells, capillary endothelial cells, loop of Henle ascending limb epithelial cells, and loop of Henle thick ascending limb epithelial cells decreases with age in the kidney.
evidence_location: Main text page 591-592; Fig. 2g
supporting_quote: Age-related changes in the kidney include a decrease in the relative abundance of mesangial cells, capillary endothelial cells, loop of Henle ascending limb epithelial cells and loop of Henle thick ascending limb epithelial cells (Fig. 2g).
key_terms: Mesangial cells: cells in the kidney glomerulus that provide structural support and regulate filtration

claim_text: The proportion of T cells in the spleen decreases with age while the relative amount of plasma cells increases.
evidence_location: Main text page 592; Fig. 2i-j
supporting_quote: In the spleen, the proportion of T cells decreases with age while the relative amount of plasma cells increases (Fig. 2i).

claim_text: The pro-inflammatory cytokine gene Il1b shows markedly increased expression with age in the liver, with the number of cells co-expressing Il1b and the Kupffer cell marker Clec4f increasing with age.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Extended Data Fig. 6f-j
supporting_quote: when co-staining with Il1b, we found that the number of cells expressing both Clec4f and Il1b increased with age (Extended Data Fig. 6h-j).
key_terms: Il1b: interleukin-1 beta gene encoding a pro-inflammatory cytokine; Clec4f: C-type lectin domain family 4 member F - a specific marker for liver Kupffer cells; Kupffer cells: liver-resident macrophages

claim_text: The number of somatic mutations increases with age across all organs examined, with tongue and bladder being the most affected tissues.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Fig. 3a-f; Extended Data Fig. 7
supporting_quote: we focused on genes that were expressed in at least 75% of cells for each age group within a particular tissue, and observed an age-related increase in the number of mutations across all of the organs we analysed (Fig. 3, Extended Data Fig. 7a, c, e)-tongue and bladder were the most affected

claim_text: The proportion of B cells belonging to a clonal family increases progressively with age, from ~9% at 3 months to ~20% at 18 months and ~38% at 24 months.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Fig. 4a; Extended Data Fig. 9a
supporting_quote: whereas most of the cells at 3 months were not part of a clone (9% were part of a clonal family), the number of B cells belonging to a clonotype doubled at 18 months (20%) when compared to 3 months, and doubled again from 18 months to 24 months (around 38%).
key_terms: Clonal family: group of B cells sharing similar receptor sequences indicating common clonal origin

claim_text: The proportion of T cells that are part of a clone increases from ~3% at 3 months to ~23% at 18 months and ~20% at 24 months, indicating increased clonality in the T cell repertoire with age.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Fig. 4b; Extended Data Fig. 9b
supporting_quote: only around 3% (55 out of 1,895) of the cells at 3 months were part of a clone. At 18 months and 24 months, this value increased to around 23% (479 out of 2,056) and around 20% (348 out of 1,780) of the cells, respectively
key_terms: Clonotype: group of T cells sharing identical or highly similar T cell receptor sequences

claim_text: Among all cell types and tissues examined, immune cells from the brain (myeloid microglial cells) and the kidney (macrophages) show significant age-related changes in transcriptomic diversity.
evidence_location: Main text page 593-594; Fig. 4c; Extended Data Figs. 10-11a-b
supporting_quote: We observed significant changes in diversity for cells of the immune system that originate from the brain and the kidney (Fig. 4c, Extended Data Fig. 11a, b).

claim_text: Young microglia transition through intermediate transcriptomic states before acquiring the gene expression signature characteristic of old microglia.
evidence_location: Main text page 594; Fig. 4d; Extended Data Fig. 11e
supporting_quote: Trajectory analysis suggests that young microglia go through an intermediate state, represented by the clusters mostly occupied by 18-month microglial cells, before acquiring the signature of old microglia (Extended Data Fig. 11e).

claim_text: Old (18- and 24-month) microglia upregulate MHC class I genes (H2-D1, H2-K1, B2m) and genes associated with degenerative disease (such as Fth1) compared to young (3-month) microglia.
evidence_location: Main text page 594; Fig. 4d-e; Supplementary Table 10
supporting_quote: These cells upregulate major histocompatibility complex (MHC) class I genes (H2-D1, H2-K1 and B2m), along with genes associated with degenerative disease (for example, Fth1).
key_terms: MHC class I: major histocompatibility complex class I molecules involved in antigen presentation; Fth1: ferritin heavy chain 1 gene associated with iron storage and neurodegeneration

claim_text: Kidney macrophages segregate into two transcriptomically distinct populations with age-dependent composition: one enriched for anti-inflammatory genes and predominantly composed of young cells, and another enriched for pro-inflammatory genes and predominantly composed of old cells.
evidence_location: Main text page 594; Fig. 4f; Extended Data Fig. 11f
supporting_quote: Regarding kidney macrophages, we found two clusters for which the composition changed markedly with age. Cluster 10 is primarily composed of cells from 1-month-old and 3-month-old mice, whereas cluster 13 is mostly composed of cells from 18-month-old, 21-month-old, 24-month-old and 30-month-old mice (Fig. 4f).

claim_text: The expression level of Cdkn2a (p16) in cells that express it approximately doubled with age.
evidence_location: Main text page 590-591; Fig. 2c-d
supporting_quote: this was accompanied by a twofold increase in the expression levels of p16 in cells in which it was expressed (Fig. 2c, d).
key_terms: p16: protein product of Cdkn2a; a cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor and senescence marker

claim_text: In 30-month-old mice, the fraction of cells expressing Cdkn2a (p16) was smaller than in 24-month-old mice.
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Fig. 2b
supporting_quote: Notably, in 30-month-old mice the fraction of cells that expressed p16 was smaller than in 24-month-old mice, perhaps because long-living animals have a slower rate of senescence.

claim_text: Among senescence-associated genes, E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf, and Itgax show positive correlation between ageing and the fraction of cells expressing them.
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Supplementary Table 4
supporting_quote: other genes with positive correlation include E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf and Itgax

claim_text: The fraction of cells expressing Sirt family genes (Sirt3, Sirt4, and Sirt5) decreases with age.
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Supplementary Table 4
supporting_quote: For some genes, including members of the Sirt family (Sirt3, Sirt4 and Sirt5), the fraction of cells in which they were expressed was found to decrease with age
key_terms: Sirt genes: sirtuin family genes encoding NAD-dependent deacetylases implicated in delaying cellular senescence

claim_text: Differential gene expression in the bladder shows stromal-associated genes (such as Col1a1, Col1a2, Col3a1, and Dcn) downregulated with age while epithelial-associated genes (such as Krt15, Krt18, and Sfn) are upregulated.
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Fig. 2f
supporting_quote: we found that stromal-associated genes (Col1a1, Col1a2, Col3a1 and Dcn) are downregulated while epithelial-associated genes (Krt15, Krt18 and Sfn) are upregulated

claim_text: The endothelial population in the bladder declines with age while the leukocyte population increases, accompanied by tissue-wide overexpression of immune-related genes including Lgals3, Igfbp2, and Ly6d.
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Fig. 2f; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: The increase in the leukocyte population could indicate an inflammatory tissue microenvironment, a common hallmark of ageing that is consistent with literature on overactive bladders and is supported by a significant overexpression of Lgals3, Igfbp2 and Ly6d across the tissue

claim_text: Old bladder leukocytes show increased expression of pro-inflammatory markers (including Cd14, Lgals3, and Tnfrsf12a) and decreased expression of anti-inflammatory markers (including Cd9 and Cd81).
evidence_location: Main text page 591; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: we observed that old leukocytes showed increased expression of pro-inflammatory markers-such as Cd14, Lgals3 and Tnfrsf12a-and decreased expression of anti-inflammatory markers such as Cd9 and Cd81

claim_text: Tissue-wide expression of Egf and Atp1a1 in the kidney decreases with age, suggesting impaired glomerular filtration.
evidence_location: Main text page 591-592; Fig. 2h; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: together with a tissue-wide reduction of Egf and Atp1a1 expression (Fig. 2h, Supplementary Table 6)-suggest an impaired glomerular filtration rate
key_terms: Egf: epidermal growth factor gene; Atp1a1: gene encoding the alpha-1 subunit of the sodium/potassium ATPase

claim_text: Local Atp1a1 expression increases with age in both capillary endothelial cells and mesangial cells in the kidney, suggesting a compensatory mechanism counteracting the declining proportion of these cell types.
evidence_location: Main text page 591-592; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: Notably, local Atp1a1 expression increases with age in both capillary endothelial cells and mesangial cells, suggesting that a compensation mechanism counteracts the effects of the declining proportion of these cells with age.

claim_text: Expression of Umod (encoding uromodulin) decreases across the kidney tissue with age due to the reduced proportion of ascending limb epithelial cells that produce it.
evidence_location: Main text page 592; Fig. 2g; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: the expression of Umod-which encodes uromodulin, the most abundant protein in urine-is also reduced across the tissue
key_terms: Umod: gene encoding uromodulin (Tamm-Horsfall protein) - the most abundant protein in normal urine

claim_text: Umod expression increases within the ascending limb epithelial cell types that become less abundant with age, paralleling the compensatory pattern observed for Atp1a1.
evidence_location: Main text page 592; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: As with Atp1a1, we see that the expression of Umod increases in a cell type that becomes less abundant with age, leading to an overall reduction of its expression in the organ.

claim_text: Age-related changes in spleen composition are supported by upregulation of B cell and plasma cell markers Cd79a and Jchain and downregulation of the T cell marker Cd3d.
evidence_location: Main text page 592; Fig. 2j; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: This is supported by the upregulation of B cell and plasma cell marker genes (Cd79a and Jchain (also known as Igj), respectively; Fig. 2j, Supplementary Table 6) and the downregulation of Cd3d
key_terms: Jchain: joining chain gene (also known as Igj) - a component of polymeric immunoglobulins and a plasma cell marker

claim_text: The T cell population in the mammary gland declines with age.
evidence_location: Main text page 592; Extended Data Fig. 5c
supporting_quote: in the mammary gland we observed a decline of the T cell population

claim_text: AP1 transcription factors (Junb, Jund, and Fos) are upregulated with age in the mammary gland, consistent with normal mammary gland involution.
evidence_location: Main text page 592-593; Extended Data Fig. 5d; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: genes encoding AP1 transcription factors (Junb, Jund and Fos) were upregulated with age (Extended Data Fig. 5d, Supplementary Table 6), consistent with the observation that normal involution of the mammary gland is accompanied by increased expression of this gene family
key_terms: AP1: activator protein 1 transcription factor complex including JUN and FOS family members

claim_text: The relative proportion of hepatocytes in the liver decreases with age, supported by a tissue-wide reduction in albumin (Alb) expression.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Extended Data Fig. 6a, 6e; Supplementary Table 5
supporting_quote: We observed an age-related decrease in the relative number of hepatocytes (Extended Data Fig. 6a-d), which is supported by the reduction in the expression of albumin (Alb; Extended Data Fig. 6e, Supplementary Table 6)

claim_text: Differential gene expression in the liver reveals an increased immune signature with age, illustrated by overexpression of H2-Aa, H2-Ab1, H2-D1, H2-Eb1, Cd74, and Lyz2.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Extended Data Fig. 6e
supporting_quote: Differential gene expression analysis revealed an increased immune signature, as illustrated by the overexpression of H2-Aa, H2-Ab1, H2-D1, H2-Eb1, Cd74, Lyz2 and others (Extended Data Fig. 6e).

claim_text: Mrc1 expression increases in liver Kupffer cells with age, whereas overall Mrc1 expression in liver endothelial cells decreases with age.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Extended Data Fig. 6k; Supplementary Table 6
supporting_quote: We identified increased expression of Mrc1 with age in Kupffer cells, whereas the overall Mrc1 in endothelial cells decreased with age (Supplementary Table 6).
key_terms: Mrc1: mannose receptor C-type 1 gene involved in immune defence; LSECs: liver sinusoidal endothelial cells

claim_text: The age-related increase in somatic mutations exceeds what is expected from amplification and sequencing errors, as verified using ERCC spike-in controls that show no corresponding age-related increase.
evidence_location: Main text page 593; Fig. 3; Extended Data Fig. 8
supporting_quote: We controlled for sequencing coverage and gene expression levels (Extended Data Fig. 8a, c, e), and verified that the number of mutations exceeded those expected from amplification and sequencing errors, which can be estimated using External RNA Controls Consortium (ERCC) controls that were spiked into each well
key_terms: ERCC: External RNA Controls Consortium - synthetic RNA spike-in controls used to assess technical variation in RNA sequencing

claim_text: Old microglia are enriched for interferon responsive and regulatory genes (including Oasl2, Ifit3, Stat1, and Irf7) compared to young microglia, suggesting expansion of a pro-inflammatory microglial subset in the ageing brain.
evidence_location: Main text page 594; Supplementary Table 10
supporting_quote: gene expression in clusters 10, 12 and 14 is enriched with interferon responsive or regulatory genes (for example, Oasl2, Oas1a, Ifit3, Rtp4, Bst2, Stat1, Irf7, Ifitm3, Usp18, Ifi204 and Ifit2), suggesting an expansion of this small pro-inflammatory subset of microglia in the ageing brain

claim_text: The kidney macrophage anti-inflammatory population is enriched for genes such as Il10, H2-Eb1, H2-Ab1, Cd74, and Cxcl16, while the pro-inflammatory population is enriched for genes such as Hp, Itgal, Msrb1, and Gngt2.
evidence_location: Main text page 594; Extended Data Fig. 11f; Supplementary Table 10
supporting_quote: Differential gene expression analysis revealed that cluster 10 is enriched for an M2-macrophage gene signature (for example, Il10, H2-Eb1, H2-Ab1, H2-Aq, Cd74, Cxcl16, Hexb, Cd81, C1qb and Cd72) whereas cluster 13 resembles a M1-proinflammatory macrophage state (for example, Hp, Itgal, Msrb1 and Gngt2)

claim_text: The relative abundance of precursor B cells in the bone marrow decreases with age.
evidence_location: Extended Data Fig. 5e; Supplementary Table 5
supporting_quote: Relative abundances of marrow precursor B cells change significantly with age

claim_text: Cells annotated as B cells or endothelial cells from different organs cluster together by whole-transcriptome analysis irrespective of tissue of origin or sequencing method, validating the consistency of cell-type annotations across the atlas.
evidence_location: Main text page 590; Fig. 1e-f; Extended Data Fig. 1g-l
supporting_quote: cells annotated as B cells or endothelial cells tend to occupy the same clusters irrespective of their tissue of origin or the method with which they were processed (Fig. 1e, f, Extended Data Fig. 1g-l).

claim_text: The relative abundance of skin keratinocyte stem cells changes significantly with age.
evidence_location: Extended Data Fig. 5g; Supplementary Table 5
supporting_quote: Relative abundances of skin keratinocyte stem cells change significantly with age

claim_text: The diversity score metric for identifying age-susceptible cell types is not confounded by the number of genes expressed per cell.
evidence_location: Extended Data Fig. 11c-d
supporting_quote: These results were not confounded by the number of genes expressed per cell (Extended Data Fig. 11c, d).

claim_text: reason_for_exclusion

claim_id: Approximately 55 of the top 200 differentially expressed genes between young and old microglial clusters overlap with a previously reported Alzheimer's disease-specific microglial gene signature.
claim_text: Requires an external gene list (the Alzheimer's disease-specific microglial signature from Keren-Shaul et al.) not among the paper's own datasets. The differential expression itself is derivable, but the overlap comparison depends on an external classification.

claim_id: Hepatocyte cell size increases and hepatocyte cell number decreases with age in the liver.
claim_text: This finding is from bright-field imaging experiments (Extended Data Fig. 6b-d), not from the scRNA-seq datasets described in the dataset profile. A researcher with access only to the scRNA-seq data could not replicate these measurements.

claim_id: The number of Mrc1-expressing liver sinusoidal endothelial cells (LSECs) increases with age, although Mrc1 expression per LSEC does not increase.
claim_text: The increased number of Mrc1-expressing LSECs was demonstrated primarily by in situ RNA staining (Extended Data Fig. 6l-o), not directly derivable from the scRNA-seq dataset alone. The negative result about per-cell expression is from scRNA-seq but is not informative on its own.

claim_id: The number of Clec4f-positive Kupffer cells does not change significantly with age in the liver.
claim_text: This specific cell count finding was quantified by in situ RNA staining (Extended Data Fig. 6g-h); while tissue composition analysis from scRNA-seq is consistent, the primary evidence for the absolute cell number claim is from staining data not in the dataset profile.

claim_id: LSECs show age-related changes in gene signatures that are closely linked with their function in immune response.
claim_text: This claim is partly interpretive and relies on literature references about LSEC function rather than being directly verifiable from the dataset alone.
